result:
Done. AM away session complete — **one masterplan step (62.1) executed under the full harness loop**, honest CONDITIONAL outcome, committed and pushed.

## Summary

**Step 0 — operator tokens:** clean no-op. `unapplied_tokens()` = 0, no `operator_tokens.jsonl` yet, no `HALT-DEV`. Nothing to apply.

**Step 1 — phase-62.1** (Slack bot under launchd, restart on current code). Selected because every other pre-departure pending step has a live/operator/time gate that blocks an unattended weekend flip (62.2 needs a live operator token round-trip; 62.6 is parked CONDITIONAL/PM-owned; 62.7 is the operator-watched Sunday rehearsal). 62.1 had the most valuable AM-actionable work.

**What happened:**
- **Research gate** revalidated (`gate_passed`, 6 sources in full, recency scan).
- **GENERATE — restart-only, zero file/plist/.env/trading edits.** The launchd cutover was already done; the only gap was criterion 2 (running bot lstart was 8s *older* than commit `1be98e83` — a pure commit-timestamp artifact; the bot already held the current code). I restarted via the **rail-9-authorized `kickstart -k`** to satisfy the immutable wording + as clean pre-departure hygiene. New PID **83982**, lstart `Sat Jun 13 07:46:26` (postdates the commit + both changed-file mtimes), single instance, old PIDs dead, KeepAlive=true, healthy Socket Mode reconnect <1s. **Criteria 1 + 2 PASS.**
- **Q/A (fresh, independent) = CONDITIONAL, ok:true.** Reproduced 1+2 live, 5/5 compliance checks pass, rails 4/6/9 clean. It confirmed I was right **not** to fake criterion 3 (a one-shot script send isn't "from the launchd bot"), and **caught a real error I'd made**: digests are weekend-gated (`scheduler.py:543`), so criterion 3 can't fire today/Sunday — **earliest qualifying digest is Monday 2026-06-15.** I corrected that date in `live_check`/`experiment_results`.
- **Logged** Cycle 64; **62.1 left `pending`** (correctly not flipped).

**Two things for you:**
1. **62.1 closes Monday 2026-06-15** — the scheduled away session captures the real `digest sent` log line + Slack permalink from the launchd bot, runs the closing Q/A, and flips. (Already owned by the calendar; no action needed from you.)
2. **FABLE-HEADLESS finding** (filed in `pending_tokens.json`, non-blocking): the `researcher`/`qa` `model: claude-fable-5` pins **fail to spawn in headless `claude -p` sessions** — I overrode each spawn to Opus 4.8 (your away-session pin + their pre-59.1 config), and both ran fine. This recurs every away session. Recommended default: keep the Opus override; reply `FABLE HEADLESS: REPIN AGENTS TO OPUS` only if you'd rather change the agent files.

**Pushed:** `b6f321d9` + changelog `dcef12f8` → `origin/main`. Rails all clean (no .env, no trading-behavior file, no force-push, main-only, $0 metered). Bot confirmed stable post-restart. Stopping here per the one-step-per-AM-session rail.
